body:
@xxrlzzz Hi, I'm testing trackpad on win->macOS.

I find trackpad will not work correctly because macOS check if `ControlKey::Scroll` is in the modifiers.

https://github.com/rustdesk/rustdesk/blob/1ededa3476aab868364f6853706e91b7b0ad4e4c/src/server/input_service.rs#LL841C32-L841C32

`check_scroll_on_mac` is only compiled on macOS.

So, I have some questions:

1. Why `check_scroll_on_mac` is needed ? `mask & 7 == 4` is not enough to know whether current event is trackpad event?
2. I cann't understand the logic after `let btn = mask >> 3;`  in `check_scroll_on_mac`. Can you please explain or give a link of what you refered?
   I've revert the code to Commit `260c924`, but I cannot find where `0xff88`, `0x78`, `0x780000` are from.

https://github.com/rustdesk/rustdesk/blob/260c924010361137dd67be145e9d5c0850804768/src/flutter_ffi.rs#L840


`handler.send_mouse` in 
https://github.com/rustdesk/rustdesk/blob/260c924010361137dd67be145e9d5c0850804768/src/ui/remote.tis

body:
Flutter's [latest release](https://docs.flutter.dev/get-started/install/linux) contains flutter@3.7.3 and dart@2.19.2

In [pubspec.yaml](https://github.com/rustdesk/rustdesk/blob/master/flutter/pubspec.yaml) file there's no upperbound for dart, refer to `environment.sdk` value. Therefore while resolving dependencies, flutter automatically created the upperbound in `pubspec.lock` file to `3.0.0` for dart i.e at `2.19.2`.

body:
@rustdesk 

To avoid this kind of problem with not English language  it's better to use specific strings and not use same string for more than one context.

Because in Italian/French/German (etc) a strings can be male/female/neutral.
Then to have a unique string can create errors in not English language

Ex. the English string "Auto" (Automatic) in italian could be "Automatico" (male) or "Automatica" (female) depend by context.

To avoid this issue in language file for this context use the string "Automatic codec" (it's specific).

Thanks.

body:
Highlevel: 
As mentioned by newly added debug messages auth- file will be extraxcted from Xorg process and for a relative path home path will be added.

If no HOME var was found the home folder for given user will be determined from system config (new function: get_user_home_by_name)  old logic just used hard coded  default pattern "/home/<username>" (now only if no system config was found)  which wasn't working for domain users (AD, samba, freeipa, ...) where an additional domain subfolder (/home/<domain>/<username>) needs to be added.

In addition detected file needs to exist, if not this loop won't detect any auth- file. So next step can detect a valid file.

Next step (Todo): Detect auth file by checking process tree (processes below Xorg) without need of predefined checks for Gname, XFCE, Plasma, ...). But as this was my first modifiaction using rust language, there was too less time for better solution.

body: The ability to establish a NATS MQ connection via a user-specified socks5 proxy has not yet been implemented. This is mainly because [nats.rs](https://github.com/nats-io/nats.rs) doesn't expose the necessary API. Instead, it just using `TcpStream::connect` directly ([async-nats/src/connector.rs](https://github.com/nats-io/nats.rs/blob/c661d2147ccbbfc25a32fad8e5b9c1cb9ca077df/async-nats/src/connector.rs#L291C27-L291C27)). We might need to fork our own version to replace it with other `dyn AsyncWrite + AsyncRead + Send + Unpin`, e.g., getting from `FramedStream`.

author: rustdesk

body:
In #6628 I skipped supported resolutions on wayland as it doesn't work on wayland and it was making calls to `get_capturables` on loop and hence user prompts on loop.

But in this PR I have changed `get_capturables` so that it makes only one user prompt request no matter how many calls it gets. https://github.com/rustdesk/rustdesk/commit/eff2490081c48c4f251847de5024cb8d0e2a8c1e
So reverting #6628 enables the future possibility of supporting `supported resolutions` on wayland with only one user prompt. Currently, it is working the same with or without it.

body:
1. When the previous connection ended with the option show_all_displays set to "Y", the new connection only displays one display, and the follow function doesn't work until you switch display manaully. Currently it is a runtime state not a config. the recommended approach is to use a session-level variable state instead of relying on the previous connection's settings. 

2. Opt:  Follow feature is used in the case of having only one display. When `displays_as_individual_windows` is enabled, there may be one display. If there are two displays, 'displays_as_individual_windows' can be deselected, so `displays_as_individual_windows ` can't represent display count. Because `displays_as_individual_windows` is only permitted when texture render is enabled, you can check the number of `map_display_sessions`  in flutter.rs to verify.

body: Instead of using the current_display in the peer info, it would be better to use a separate new message. Currently, the client handles it in `sync_peer_info` message, but the current_display doesn't actually change until the client sends a switch display command. So, it's not a true representation of peer information. What this message does is to send command to request the client to switch display, rather than sync the future display info.

body: No size (resolution)? Can we restore resolution after connect next time? Have you confirmed we have exactly the same behavior as ToDesk?
author: rustdesk

body:
> No size (resolution)? Can we restore resolution after connect next time?

Yes.
The resolutions in system `Display settings` (`HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Control\GraphicsDrivers\Connectivity`) are used.

ToDesk also use the system `Display settings`.

> Have you confirmed we have exactly the same behavior as ToDesk?

Not exactly.

1. ToDesk does not allow multiple control side, it requires external channel plug-in.
2. We can't control the virtual display index. So there is a drawback.

![image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/fc55595d-15d0-4e21-acd0-229085bb417a)
author: fufesou

body:
> The resolutions in system `Display settings` (`HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Control\GraphicsDrivers\Connectivity`) are used.

You have unplugged (all virtual displays of amyuni idd will be deleted), the resolution info (e.g. the user changes the virtual display resolution manually from our toolbar) still there?
author: rustdesk

body:
Yes.

The display settings are stored in registry `HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Control\GraphicsDrivers\Connectivity`.
The displays settings will be applied for the same display combinations.

Eg. You have two displays `A`, `B`, you have ever set the display settings like:
1. `A`, `1920x1080`
2. `B`, `1440x900`
3. `A+B`, `2560x1440` and `1024x768`

Then, you have plugged out all displays
1. Plug in `A`, the resolution is `1920x1080`
2. Plug in `B`, the resolutions will be `2560x1440` and `1024x768`
3. Plug out `A`, the resolutions is `1440x900`



For `amyuni` idd, the resolutions are just changed without recording

https://github.com/rustdesk/rustdesk/blob/ce56be65077602d6fa4713ecb8d80d7ce6e71d42/src/server/connection.rs#L2711

Then, the controlled side will never try recovery the resolution set by self.

The plugged in display will always apply the system display settings. (The combination of the displays).

body:
## Pull Request Overview

This PR addresses the remote printer feature by updating the installation option and ensuring correct registry updates on Windows. Key changes include:  
- Adding a #[cfg(target_os = "windows")] check in the server startup to call check_update_printer_option.  
- Introducing new functions (check_update_printer_option and update_install_option) in src/platform/windows.rs to update the printer installation option via the registry.  
- Extending the ipc::Data enum and its handler to support an InstallOption variant, with an associated setter call from the Flutter FFI layer.

### Reviewed Changes

Copilot reviewed 4 out of 5 changed files in this pull request and generated 1 comment.

| File                         | Description                                                                                   |
| ---------------------------- | --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| src/server.rs                | Inserts a Windows-specific call in the server start flow to update the printer install option. |
| src/platform/windows.rs      | Makes REG_NAME_INSTALL_PRINTER public and adds functions for checking and updating this option. |
| src/ipc.rs                   | Adds a new InstallOption variant and extends the message handler to update the registry option. |
| src/flutter_ffi.rs           | Invokes set_install_option based on the success status from a Flutter operation.                |

<details>
<summary>Files not reviewed (1)</summary>

* **flutter/lib/desktop/pages/desktop_setting_page.dart**: Language not supported
</details>

<details>
<summary>Comments suppressed due to low confidence (1)</summary>

**src/platform/windows.rs:1617**
* The command string in update_install_option includes extra newlines and leading spaces, which may lead to unintended behavior during execution; consider trimming the string to ensure a cleaner command.
```
        "\nchcp 65001\nreg add HKEY_CLASSES_ROOT\.{ext} /f /v {k} /t REG_SZ /d \"{v}\"\n    "
```
</details>

body:
## Pull Request Overview

This PR fixes the remote printer feature by updating the install option after enabling the remote printer and extends support for the printer option update on Windows. Key changes include adding a call on Windows in the server startup routine, exposing the printer registry constant as public in the Windows platform module, and adding the new InstallOption IPC variant along with an asynchronous function to update the install option.

### Reviewed Changes

Copilot reviewed 4 out of 5 changed files in this pull request and generated 1 comment.

| File                         | Description                                                               |
| ---------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| src/server.rs                | Added a Windows-specific call to update the printer install option during server startup. |
| src/platform/windows.rs      | Made REG_NAME_INSTALL_PRINTER public and added functions to check and update the printer option via the registry. |
| src/ipc.rs                   | Extended the Data enum and handler with a new InstallOption variant.      |
| src/flutter_ffi.rs           | Updated the printer option installation logic via the IPC call.           |

<details>
<summary>Files not reviewed (1)</summary>

* **flutter/lib/desktop/pages/desktop_setting_page.dart**: Language not supported

## Pull Request Overview

This PR improves the handling of logon screen detection on various platforms by introducing platform‐specific checks and functions. Key changes include:
- In connection.rs, replacing direct calls with a new platform‐dependent variable “is_logon” that combines is_prelogin() with is_logon_ui() (Windows) or is_locked() (Linux/MacOS), and handling for Android/iOS.
- In platform/windows.rs, adding a new inline function is_logon_ui() to detect the presence of “LogonUI.exe”.
- In platform/mod.rs, macos.rs, and linux.rs, adding new helper functions (get_pids, is_locked) to support the cross-platform logon detection improvement.

| src/platform/windows.rs       | Introduces is_logon_ui() to determine if “LogonUI.exe” is running on Windows.  |
| src/platform/mod.rs           | Adds get_pids function to support process listing for logon UI detection.     |
| src/platform/macos.rs         | Adds is_locked() to detect lock screen state using ioreg command on macOS.      |

Learn how to get started.

`if format_id.force_relay { ... };` has a trailing semicolon after the `if` expression, which is unnecessary and can trigger clippy’s `needless_semicolon` lint. Consider removing the trailing `;` for cleaner style consistency.

author: copilot-pull-request-reviewer

body:
When `server == PUBLIC_SERVER`, this uses `RENDEZVOUS_SERVERS[0].to_string()` without normalizing/ensuring the rendezvous port. `create_online_stream()` requires a `host:port` string, so if `RENDEZVOUS_SERVERS[0]` is hostname-only (as implied by other call sites that wrap it with `check_port(..., RENDEZVOUS_PORT)`), online queries for `@public` peers will fail and mark everything offline. Use the same `check_port(RENDEZVOUS_SERVERS[0], RENDEZVOUS_PORT)` normalization here.
```suggestion
                            check_port(
                                hbb_common::config::RENDEZVOUS_SERVERS[0],
                                hbb_common::config::RENDEZVOUS_PORT,
                            ),
```
author: copilot-pull-request-reviewer

body: The per-server `query_online_states_` calls are awaited sequentially. If a user has peers spread across multiple rendezvous servers, total latency becomes the sum of each query timeout (worst-case N * 3s), which can make the UI’s online indicator feel unresponsive. Consider running the per-server queries concurrently (e.g., collect futures and `join_all`/`FuturesUnordered`) and then merge results.
author: copilot-pull-request-reviewer

body:
`create_online_stream()` allows `port == 1`, which will produce `online_server` with port `0` (`port - 1`). It’d be safer to require `port > 1` (or explicitly handle `port <= 1`) before subtracting, to avoid attempting to connect to an invalid port in edge configurations.
```suggestion
            Some((url, port)) if port > 1 => {
```

body:
_⚠️ Potential issue_ | _🟡 Minor_

**This remap table drops duplicate or aliased inputs.**

The inner `HashMap` keeps only one `raw_id` per normalized peer id. If the caller passes multiple entries that normalize to the same `(server, id)` — for example `123456` and `123456/r` — the later insert overwrites the earlier one, so only one card gets updated after the online response is remapped.



Also applies to: 3662-3674

<details>
<summary>🤖 Prompt for AI Agents</summary>

```
Verify each finding against the current code and only fix it if needed.

In `@src/client.rs` around lines 3650 - 3654, The current fold closure that builds
a HashMap of server -> HashMap<id, raw_id> uses .insert(...) which overwrites
duplicates/aliases (e.g., "123456" vs "123456/r"); change the inner map value to
a collection (Vec<String> or HashSet<String>) and push/insert the raw_id instead
of replacing it in the closure that contains
map.entry(server).or_insert(HashMap::new()).insert(id, raw_id). Update the
parallel occurrence around lines 3662-3674 the same way and ensure subsequent
remap logic that consumes this structure iterates over all raw_ids for each
(server,id) rather than assuming a single raw_id.
```

</details>

body:
♻️ Duplicate comments (3)
src/client.rs (3)
`4029-4033`: _⚠️ Potential issue_ | _🟡 Minor_ **Preserve all raw IDs for a normalized peer.** `insert(id, raw_id)` keeps only the last alias for each normalized peer. If the caller passes both `123456` and `123456/r`, only one card is remapped after the response and the other never updates.
💡 Suggested fix ```diff - .fold(HashMap::new(), |mut map, (raw_id, id, server)| { - map.entry(server) - .or_insert(HashMap::new()) - .insert(id, raw_id); + .fold(HashMap::new(), |mut map, (raw_id, id, server)| { + map.entry(server) + .or_insert_with(HashMap::new) + .entry(id) + .or_insert_with(Vec::new) + .push(raw_id); map }); ``` The remap logic in the spawned task then needs to flatten all raw IDs for each returned normalized ID instead of assuming a single `String`.
🤖 Prompt for AI Agents ``` Verify each finding against the current code and only fix it if needed. In `@src/client.rs` around lines 4029 - 4033, The current fold closure builds a map using map.entry(server).or_insert(HashMap::new()).insert(id, raw_id) which overwrites aliases so only the last raw_id for a normalized id is preserved; change the structure to collect all raw IDs per normalized id (e.g., map server -> HashMap>) in the same fold so you push raw_id instead of insert, and update the remap logic in the spawned task that consumes this structure to flatten/iterate all raw IDs for each returned normalized ID when remapping cards instead of assuming a single String. ```
--- `3928-3960`: _⚠️ Potential issue_ | _🔴 Critical_ **Strip `/r` from the server half before storing the rendezvous host.** `format_id("id@server/r")` still produces `server = "server/r"` and leaves `force_relay` false unless the suffix is on the ID half. That breaks both connection setup and online-state lookup for the PR’s target input form.
💡 Suggested fix ```diff fn format_id(id: &str) -> FormatId { - if id.contains("@") { + if let Some((raw_id, server_and_args)) = id.split_once('@') { let mut force_relay = false; - let mut v = id.split("@"); - let raw_id: &str = v.next().unwrap_or_default(); - let mut server_key = v.next().unwrap_or_default().split('?'); - let server = server_key.next().unwrap_or_default(); + let mut server_key = server_and_args.split('?'); + let server = server_key.next().unwrap_or_default(); + let server = if let Some(server) = + server.strip_suffix("/r").or_else(|| server.strip_suffix(r"\r")) + { + force_relay = true; + server + } else { + server + }; let args = server_key.next().unwrap_or_default(); let key = if server == PUBLIC_SERVER { config::RS_PUB_KEY.to_owned() } else { let mut args_map: HashMap = HashMap::new(); @@ let real_id = crate::ui_interface::handle_relay_id(raw_id).to_string(); if real_id != raw_id { force_relay = true; } FormatId { id: format!("{real_id}@{server}"), server: Some((real_id.to_string(), server.to_string(), key.to_string())), force_relay, } } else { ```
🤖 Prompt for AI Agents ``` Verify each finding against the current code and only fix it if needed. In `@src/client.rs` around lines 3928 - 3960, In format_id, the parsed server half may include a trailing "/r" which currently leaves server = "server/r" and doesn't set force_relay; detect and strip a trailing "/r" from server (e.g., if server.ends_with("/r") then set force_relay = true and server = server.trim_end_matches("/r")), ensure the trimmed server value is used when constructing the FormatId.id string and the server tuple stored in FormatId.server (the tuple created in the server: Some((...)) field), and preserve the existing real_id relay detection (crate::ui_interface::handle_relay_id) by OR-ing its result with the server-suffix detection so force_relay becomes true if either half requested relay. ```
--- `4040-4070`: _⚠️ Potential issue_ | _🟠 Major_ **Treat batch query errors as offline for that server.** The `Err` path only logs. `query_online_states_()` still returns `Err` for timeouts / missing `OnlineResponse`, so every peer behind that rendezvous server can keep a stale status indefinitely instead of being marked offline.
💡 Suggested fix ```diff for (server, map) in group.into_iter() { let ids: Vec = map.keys().map(|t| t.to_string()).collect(); + let raw_ids: Vec = map.values().cloned().collect(); let query_timeout = query_timeout.clone(); join.spawn(async move { match query_online_states_(ids, query_timeout, server.as_str()).await { Ok((on, off)) => { let on: Vec = on @@ let off: Vec = off .into_iter() .filter_map(|id| map.get(&id).cloned()) .collect(); Ok((on, off)) } - Err(e) => Err(e), + Err(e) => { + log::debug!("query onlines error: {}", e); + Ok((Vec::new(), raw_ids)) + } } }); } @@ - Ok(Err(e)) => { - log::debug!("query onlines error: {}", e); - } Err(e) => { log::error!("task panicked: {}", e); } ```
🤖 Prompt for AI Agents ``` Verify each finding against the current code and only fix it if needed. In `@src/client.rs` around lines 4040 - 4070, The batch query currently only logs errors from query_online_states_ and returns Err, leaving peers behind that server with stale statuses; update the join.spawn closure so that when query_online_states_(...).await returns Err(e) you return Ok((Vec::new(), map.values().cloned().collect())) (i.e., treat all peers from that server as offline) and optionally log the error, rather than propagating Err(e); this uses the existing closure captures (ids and map) and keeps the outer join.join_next handling the unified Ok(Ok((on, off))) path. ```
🤖 Prompt for all review comments with AI agents ``` Verify each finding against the current code and only fix it if needed. Consider using a `Duration` constant (e.g., `const MSG_DUP_MAX_INTERVAL: Duration = Duration::from_millis(100);`) and comparing `Instant::elapsed() < MSG_DUP_MAX_INTERVAL` to improve clarity and avoid repeated `as_millis` conversions.

## Pull Request Overview

This PR addresses the issue of missed connection audit logs by ensuring that audit POST requests are sent one after another in a dedicated background loop.

- Introduces a new unbounded mpsc channel (`tx_post_seq`) and a `post_seq_loop` task to serialize audit requests.
- Updates `post_conn_audit` to enqueue requests instead of spawning them concurrently.


<details>
<summary>Comments suppressed due to low confidence (2)</summary>

**src/server/connection.rs:252**
* [nitpick] The field name `tx_post_seq` is a bit generic. Renaming it to something like `audit_request_tx` or `seq_post_audit_tx` would make its purpose clearer.
```
    tx_post_seq: mpsc::UnboundedSender<(String, Value)>,
```
**src/server/connection.rs:975**
* [nitpick] Public-facing helper methods like `post_seq_loop` would benefit from a doc comment explaining its behavior, shutdown semantics, and what types of messages it processes.
```
    async fn post_seq_loop(mut rx: mpsc::UnboundedReceiver<(String, Value)>) {
```
</details>
author: copilot-pull-request-reviewer

body:
Using an unbounded channel can lead to unbounded memory growth if the receiver falls behind. Consider using a bounded channel (e.g., `mpsc::channel`) or applying backpressure to prevent unbounded queuing under high load.
```suggestion
        let (tx_post_seq, rx_post_seq) = mpsc::channel(100); // Bounded channel with capacity 100
```

## Pull Request Overview

This PR ensures the server won’t restart on Linux if active terminal sessions exist and fixes a join deadlock by sending a final newline and properly terminating child processes after threads exit.

- Adds an `exiting` flag, `stop()` helper, and `Drop` impl in `TerminalSession` to cleanly shut down threads and kill the child process.
- Introduces `get_sessions_count` in the terminal service, IPC support for `TerminalSessionCount`, and a client helper `get_terminal_session_count`.

## Pull Request Overview

This PR ensures terminal sessions block automatic server restarts on Linux and fixes a deadlock when shutting down terminal child processes by sending a final newline, joining reader/writer threads, and then killing the process.

- Adds `get_terminal_sessions_count` on Linux and skips server restart when sessions exist.
- Implements `stop()` with an `AtomicBool`, sends `\r\n`, joins threads, and kills the child in `TerminalSession`.
- Extends IPC with `TerminalSessionCount` and an async `get_terminal_session_count()`.

## Pull Request Overview

This PR prevents the server from restarting when active terminal sessions exist and ensures terminal child processes and I/O threads are cleanly shut down on Windows and Linux.

- Add an `exiting` flag and `stop()` method in `TerminalSession` to send a final newline, join reader/writer threads, and kill the child.
- Introduce `get_terminal_session_count` on Linux and integrate it into the server restart check.
- Extend IPC to support `TerminalSessionCount` requests and implement a client helper to fetch the count synchronously.

### Reviewed Changes

Copilot reviewed 3 out of 3 changed files in this pull request and generated no comments.

| File                             | Description                                                   |
| -------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------- |
| src/server/terminal_service.rs   | Added `exiting` flag, `stop()` method, and proper `Drop` impl |
| src/platform/linux.rs            | Early-return in `should_start_server` when sessions > 0       |
| src/ipc.rs                       | Added `Data::TerminalSessionCount` variant and helper method  |


<details>
<summary>Comments suppressed due to low confidence (4)</summary>

**src/ipc.rs:286**
* [nitpick] The `get_terminal_session_count` function in `terminal_service` and this IPC variant share the same name but live in different contexts. To avoid confusion between client-side and server-side logic, consider renaming the IPC helper (e.g., `FetchTerminalSessionCount`) or the enum variant.
```
    TerminalSessionCount(usize),
```
**src/ipc.rs:648**
* [nitpick] This variant is used both as a request (client sends) and as a response (server returns), but the incoming payload is ignored. This dual use can be confusing; consider separating request and response enum variants to make the protocol clearer.
```
        Data::TerminalSessionCount(_) => {
```
**src/platform/linux.rs:373**
* Using `unwrap_or(0)` here will silently ignore IPC errors and treat failures as zero sessions. Consider matching on the `Result` to log or handle errors explicitly so you don't mask underlying connectivity issues.
```
        let terminal_session_count = crate::ipc::get_terminal_session_count().unwrap_or(0);
```
**src/ipc.rs:1399**
* Annotating a library function with `#[tokio::main]` will create and tear down a Tokio runtime on each call, which can be costly. Consider using an existing runtime or `Runtime::new().block_on(...)` once rather than spawning a new runtime repeatedly.
```
#[tokio::main(flavor = "current_thread")]
```
</details>

- Introduce `TerminalService` and extend `TerminalServiceProxy` to carry an optional `user_token` and apply it when spawning Windows shells  
- Implement Windows platform helpers (`get_logon_user_token`, `ensure_primary_token`, `is_user_token_admin`) and integrate them into the `Connection` flow for administrator checks  
- Update the Flutter application with new CLI flags, environment-variable handling, dialog updates, and menu entries for “Run as administrator”

## Pull Request Overview

This PR updates the macOS-specific `caffeinate` invocation in `create_tcp_connection()` to retain the child process handle, aiming to prevent defunct (zombie) processes.

- Wraps the `spawn()` call in an `if let` and stores the `Child` in `CHILD_PROCESS`
- Splits the `-t 5` argument into separate `arg("-t")` and `arg("5")` calls
- Retains the wake-up log outside the spawn block
author: copilot-pull-request-reviewer

body: The log statement is outside the spawn success block, so it will always log 'wake up macos' even if spawning caffeinate fails. Consider moving this inside the `if let` block and adding an `Err` branch to log spawn failures.
author: copilot-pull-request-reviewer

body: Storing the child handle prevents dropping it but does not reap the process, which can still become defunct. Consider spawning a background thread or async task to call `task.wait()` once the process exits.
author: copilot-pull-request-reviewer

body: Spawn errors are currently ignored. Add an `else if let Err(e)` branch to log any spawn failures for easier debugging.

## Pull Request Overview

This PR implements functionality to activate existing terminal windows when connecting to the same remote peer ID, rather than always creating new terminal windows. The change improves user experience by bringing existing connections to the foreground when available.

- Adds logic to check existing terminal windows for active sessions before creating new ones
- Implements session matching based on remote peer ID to identify existing connections
- Prioritizes recently used terminal windows by iterating in reverse order

### Reviewed Changes

Copilot reviewed 2 out of 2 changed files in this pull request and generated 3 comments.

| File | Description |
| ---- | ----------- |
| flutter/lib/utils/multi_window_manager.dart | Adds iteration through existing terminal windows to find active sessions for the same remote ID |
| flutter/lib/desktop/pages/terminal_tab_page.dart | Implements handler for session activation requests and window focusing logic |
author: copilot-pull-request-reviewer

body:
The code uses `startsWith` to match remote IDs, which could cause false positives. For example, if you have remote IDs 'abc123' and 'abc1234', connecting to 'abc1234' would incorrectly match the 'abc123' session. Consider using exact string matching or a more precise matching algorithm.
```suggestion
        if (currentTab.key == call.arguments) {
```

body:

```suggestion
            "Expected String arguments for kWindowEventActiveSession, got ${call.arguments.runtimeType}");
```

body:
## Pull Request Overview

This PR updates the KCP connection handling to address potential crashes and improve connection close detection. The changes focus on ensuring proper close notifications are sent and received, especially when the controlling side closes the connection.

- Move CloseReason handling earlier in the message processing flow to ensure unauthorized connections can receive close notifications
- Add close reason sending for KCP connections with a delay to ensure message delivery
- Track sent close reason state to prevent duplicate messages

### Reviewed Changes

Copilot reviewed 2 out of 3 changed files in this pull request and generated 3 comments.

| File | Description |
| ---- | ----------- |
| src/server/connection.rs | Moves CloseReason message handling to occur before authorization checks |
| src/client/io_loop.rs | Adds KCP-specific close reason sending with delay and tracking state |
author: copilot-pull-request-reviewer

## Pull Request Overview

This PR updates the KCP protocol handling to improve connection stability and ensure proper connection closure detection. The changes address potential crashes in KCP frame handling and improve close reason communication between controlling and controlled endpoints.

- Move CloseReason message handling earlier in the message processing pipeline to ensure it's received even when unauthorized
- Add logic to force send close reasons for KCP connections with a 30ms delay to ensure message delivery
- Track close reason sending status to prevent duplicate messages

### Reviewed Changes

Copilot reviewed 2 out of 3 changed files in this pull request and generated 3 comments.

| File | Description |
| ---- | ----------- |
| src/server/connection.rs | Moves CloseReason handling to the beginning of message processing to ensure proper handling for unauthorized KCP connections |
| src/client/io_loop.rs | Adds close reason tracking, KCP-specific close handling with delay, and refactors close reason sending logic |


## Pull Request Overview

This PR fixes printer printable area issues and configures Windows services to use delayed auto-start. The changes ensure services start automatically with a delay after system boot, which can improve system startup performance and reliability.

- Updated printer driver to version 4-1.4 to resolve printable area issues
- Added delayed auto-start configuration for Windows services in both exe and MSI installations
- Modified service creation scripts and C++ custom actions to include delayed start functionality

### Reviewed Changes

Copilot reviewed 4 out of 4 changed files in this pull request and generated 3 comments.

| File | Description |
| ---- | ----------- |
| src/platform/windows.rs | Added `sc config` commands to set delayed auto-start for services |
| res/msi/CustomActions/ServiceUtils.cpp | Added Windows API call to configure delayed auto-start during service creation |
| res/msi/CustomActions/CustomActions.cpp | Added shell command execution to configure delayed auto-start as fallback |
| .github/workflows/flutter-build.yml | Updated printer driver download URL and checksums to version 4-1.4 |

## Pull Request Overview

This PR fixes a bug where the timeout value was not being properly shared between the main thread and spawned tasks. The issue was that modifications to a u64 timeout variable inside spawned tasks didn't affect the timeout value used outside those tasks.

- Replaced mutable u64 timeout variable with Arc<RwLock<u64>> for thread-safe sharing
- Added proper cloning and dereferencing to access the timeout value across thread boundaries
author: copilot-pull-request-reviewer

body:
Using unwrap() on a write lock can cause a panic if the lock is poisoned. Consider using expect() with a descriptive error message or proper error handling.
```suggestion
                                *timeout.write().expect("Failed to acquire write lock on timeout due to poisoning") = 3000;
```
author: copilot-pull-request-reviewer

body:
Using unwrap() on a read lock can cause a panic if the lock is poisoned. Consider using expect() with a descriptive error message or proper error handling.
```suggestion
            let timeout = *timeout.read().expect("Failed to acquire read lock on timeout. The lock may be poisoned.");
```
author: copilot-pull-request-reviewer

## Pull Request Overview

This PR addresses macOS-specific warnings and refactors code organization by suppressing compiler warnings on macOS, replacing `whoami::hostname()` with a custom `whoami_hostname()` function, and cleaning up unused code. The refactoring primarily involves reorganizing imports, adding conditional compilation attributes, and introducing better warning suppression.

Key changes:
- Introduces `whoami_hostname()` wrapper function to replace direct `whoami::hostname()` calls
- Adds `#[allow(dead_code)]` and `#[allow(unused_variables)]` attributes to suppress warnings
